findings.title: Parser OOB write primitive found but ROP chain construction infeasible
findings.statement: The document parser contains a controlled OOB write from a malformed length field. The write reaches a vtable pointer, but exploiting it requires a ROP chain under CFI constraints. No gadget search capability exists in the current tooling, and manual objdump inspection of a large stripped binary is insufficient to find CFI-compatible gadgets.

evidence.summary: Ghidra decompilation confirms OOB write primitive in parser; objdump shows CFI enforcement on indirect calls; no automated gadget search available
